questions.text: Consider a Hydrogen-1 atom consisting of one proton and one electron. Assume its radius is about 1 angstrom (10^-10 meters). The two particles experience gravitational force and electrostatic force, both attractive. What is the approximate ratio of the magnitude of gravitational force to the magnitude of electrostatic force.
Based on the above analysis, what can you conclude about the above situation?

answerChoices.text: In determining the structure of the atom, the gravitational force is negligible.
In determining the structure of the atom, the electrostatic force is negligible.
In determining the structure of teh atom, both forces must be considered.
Both forces are negligible; a third force is the crucial force is determining the structure of the atom.
